change_request: Replace static lib/learn.ts article data with a dynamically fetched JSON file from Google Cloud Storage so new articles can be published without redeploying
acceptance_criteria: learn index page fetches articles from GCS at request time with revalidate 60s
learn slug page fetches article by slug from GCS with revalidate 60s
GCS fetch failure returns empty list or notFound without crashing
lib/learn.ts retains only interfaces and fetch functions with no data arrays
tests cover success and failure paths for getArticles getArticle and getArticleSlugs

scope.change_request: Replace static lib/learn.ts article data with a dynamically fetched JSON file from Google Cloud Storage so new articles can be published without redeploying
acceptance_criteria: learn index page fetches articles from GCS at request time with revalidate 60s
learn slug page fetches article by slug from GCS with revalidate 60s
GCS fetch failure returns empty list or notFound without crashing
lib/learn.ts retains only interfaces and fetch functions with no data arrays
tests cover success and failure paths for getArticles getArticle and getArticleSlugs

llm_analysis.assessment: The changes successfully replace the static article data with dynamically fetched data from GCS. The migration script, GCS fetch implementation, and test updates align with the change request.

risks: Network errors during GCS fetch could lead to a degraded user experience. app/[lang]/learn/page.tsx:getArticles
Incorrectly formatted JSON in GCS could cause parsing errors. lib/learn.ts:getArticles
Changes to the GCS URL could break the application if not updated in the environment variables. .env.example

llm_analysis.notes: Error handling includes returning an empty list or notFound in case of GCS fetch failure. Null input is not explicitly handled, but the typescript compiler should catch these issues.

The data fetching functions now return promises and fetch from an external source. The shape of the Article interface remains the same, so no explicit migration is needed, but consumers need to await the results.

llm_analysis.recommendation: The tests cover success and failure paths for fetching articles from GCS. Consider adding tests for different error scenarios (e.g., network timeout, invalid JSON).

rollback.head: bc803d2e8a0f4ef3c37141de3d375c6cb9a37137
rollback_steps: 2. Resolve any merge conflicts if prompted
rollback.additional_cleanup: Review affected files for any required data/config cleanup

overall.summary: The changes successfully migrate article data to GCS, improving content management. The implementation includes error handling and tests, addressing potential issues. Ensure the GCS_ARTICLES_URL environment variable is properly configured in all environments.
